title: The Culture Map
subtitle: Breaking Through the Invisible Boundaries of Global Business
author: Erin Meyer
excerpt: A practical framework for decoding how cultural differences impact business interactions across eight critical dimensions — from communication and feedback to leadership and decision-making.
keyIdeas: Culture is invisible but profoundly shapes business behavior
Eight scales map cultural differences across key dimensions
The same behavior means different things in different cultures
Cultural intelligence is learnable, not innate
Effective cross-cultural leaders adapt their style, not their values
keyTakeaways: Trust is built differently in task-based vs. relationship-based cultures
Direct vs. indirect feedback cultures create enormous misunderstanding
Hierarchical vs. egalitarian cultures disagree on what good leadership looks like
Consensus vs. top-down decision-making varies dramatically across cultures
whoShouldRead: Anyone working on multicultural or distributed teams
whoShouldSkip: Those who work in monocultural environments
Anyone who thinks culture does not matter in business
